manual_entries.title: Zone Group Definition and Mode Synchronization
manual_entries.summary: Each system is divided into Zone Groups (collections of zones served by a single air handler). All zones in a Zone Group must be in the same zone-group operating mode; if one zone enters a mode other than Unoccupied (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that group enter that mode. Occupied-standby is a zonal subset of Occupied Mode and is not a zone-group mode.

structured_payload.title: Zone Group Definition and Mode Synchronization
structured_payload.summary: Each system is divided into Zone Groups (collections of zones served by a single air handler). All zones in a Zone Group must be in the same zone-group operating mode; if one zone enters a mode other than Unoccupied (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that group enter that mode. Occupied-standby is a zonal subset of Occupied Mode and is not a zone-group mode.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Any zone in a Zone Group is placed in a zone-group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload.required_behavior: All zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone-group operating mode as defined in Section 5.4.6. If one zone in a Zone Group is placed in any zone-group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode.

chunk.cleaned_text:
From the point of view of the BAS, individual
zone schedules are superimposed to create a zone-group  schedule, which then drives system behavior.
The schedule may govern operation of other integrated systems such as lights, daylighting, or other , in addition to
the HVAC system.
5.4.3.  All zones in each Zone Group  shall be in the same zone -group operating mode  as defined in Section 5.4.6 .
If one zone in a Zone Group  is placed in any zone -group operating mode  other than Unoccupied Mode
(due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy ), all zones in that Zone Gro up shall enter that
mode.
Occupied -standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode , and shall not
be considered a zone-group operating mode .
5.4.4.  A Zone Group  may be in only one mode at a given time.
5.4.5.  For each Zone Group , provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones
served by the Zone Group .

manual_entries.title: Zone Group Occupied Mode Conditions
manual_entries.summary: A Zone Group is in Occupied Mode when either (a) the time is between the scheduled occupied start and stop times, or (b) the schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.

structured_payload.title: Zone Group Occupied Mode Conditions
structured_payload.summary: A Zone Group is in Occupied Mode when either (a) the time is between the scheduled occupied start and stop times, or (b) the schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Time between scheduled occupied start and stop, or occupant override active.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Zone Group enters Occupied Mode.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: A Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode when any of the following is true: a. The time of day is between the Zone Group’s scheduled occupied start and stop times. b. The schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.

manual_entries.title: Active Airflow Setpoint Endpoints by Zone Group Mode
manual_entries.summary: Endpoints for active airflow setpoint vary by zone group mode: Occupied uses Vcool-max for cooling max, Vmin* for min, Vheat-max for heating max; Cooldown and Setup use Vcool-max for cooling max, 0 for min and heating max; Warmup uses 0 for cooling max and min, Vcool-max for heating max; Setback uses 0 for cooling max and min, Vcool-max for heating max; Unoccupied uses 0 for all.

manual_entries.title: Cooling-Only VAV Zone Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: Active airflow setpoint is determined by zone state: cooling maps cooling loop output from minimum to cooling maximum; deadband sets to minimum; heating maps heating loop output from minimum to heating maximum. Damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Cooling-Only VAV Zone Control Logic
structured_payload.summary: Active airflow setpoint is determined by zone state: cooling maps cooling loop output from minimum to cooling maximum; deadband sets to minimum; heating maps heating loop output from minimum to heating maximum. Damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone state changes (cooling, deadband, heating)
structured_payload.required_behavior: When cooling: map cooling-loop output to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature > room temperature, setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint. When deadband: setpoint = minimum endpoint. When heating: map heating-loop output to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature < room temperature + 3°C (5°F), setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint. Modulate damper to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.

chunk.cleaned_text:
72 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.5.5.1.  When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow
setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint .
a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active
airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .
5.5.5.2.  When the Zone State  is deadband , the active airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
5.5.5.3.  When the Zone State  is heating , the Heating Loop  output shall be mapped to the active airflow
setpoint  from  the minim um endpoint  to the heating maximum endpoint .
a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room
temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
5.5.5.4.  The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the
active setpoint .

manual_entries.title: VAV Reheat Zone Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: When Zone State is cooling, map cooling-loop output to active airflow setpoint from cooling minimum to cooling maximum endpoint; heating coil disabled unless DAT below minimum setpoint. When deadband, active airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint; heating coil disabled unless DAT below minimum setpoint. When heating, from 0-50% heating-loop output resets DAT setpoint from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temp, airflow at heating minimum endpoint; from 51-100%, if DAT > room temp + 3°C (5°F), reset airflow from heating minimum to heating maximum endpoint. Heating coil modulated to maintain DAT setpoint.

necessary to add or delete fan heat correction factors.
